name: Machaca
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Canned diced mild green chilies
1 cup Canned diced tomatoes in puree or liquid
2 lb Beef top round, trimmed of all visible fat and cut into 4 or 5 large pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large, heavy pan with a lid, over medium heat, sauté onions, chilies, red chile flakes, Tabasco, black pepper, and cumin. Add only a small amount of water as needed to prevent sticking. Stir frequently.
2. Cook until the onion is translucent and tender.
3. Add tomatoes with their liquid and stir well. Cook for 3-5 minutes.
4. Add beef. Reduce heat to simmer, cover pot, and cook, stirring occasionally, for 1-1/2 hours or until beef is thoroughly tender.
5. When beef has cooled, refrigerate overnight.
6. The next day, shred the beef pieces using fingers. Skim off any fat from the cooking juices and return beef to pan. Reheat over low heat and serve.
This is especially good as a change from bacon or sausage for breakfast or brunch, and is also good served rolled in warmed flour tortillas for lunch or dinner.
